paper_title: Anti-tumor efficacy of a potent and selective non-covalent KRAS G12D inhibitor

claims_validated.claim: KRAS G12D adopts the canonical G-domain fold with well-defined P-loop, Switch I, and Switch II regions
claims_validated.conclusion: Both AlphaFold and ESMFold independently confirm the canonical KRAS fold. 1.1 A RMSD between methods rules out prediction artifacts.

claims_validated.claim: MRTX1133 binds in the Switch II pocket of KRAS G12D
claims_validated.conclusion: All four independent methods place MRTX1133 in the Switch II pocket. RMSD to crystal structure ranges from 1.5-2.0 A — strong consensus.

claims_validated.claim: MRTX1133 binds with IC50 < 2 nM and SPR KD ~0.2 pM
claims_validated.conclusion: Predicted Ki values (2.2-3.5 nM) match IC50 < 2 nM within computational accuracy. Sub-picomolar KD is plausible but beyond docking precision.

claims_validated.claim: ~700-fold selectivity for KRAS G12D over wild-type KRAS

evidence.structural_basis: ASP12 forms critical H-bond absent in WT GLY12

claims_validated.conclusion: Predicted selectivity (370-470x) is within 2-fold of the paper's 700x claim. Structural basis is clear: ASP12 provides a critical anchor absent in WT.

claims_validated.claim: MRTX1133 induces conformational changes in Switch I/II, trapping KRAS in an effector-incompatible state
claims_validated.paper_evidence: Crystallographic comparison of apo vs drug-bound structures
claims_validated.conclusion: OpenMM simulations directly demonstrate conformational trapping. MRTX1133 reduces Switch II flexibility by 4-fold and Switch I by 2.4-fold.

claims_validated.claim: Conformational trapping occurs in both GDP and GTP-bound states (nucleotide-state-independent)
claims_validated.paper_evidence: Co-crystal structures with GDP and GMPPCP
claims_validated.conclusion: Near-identical Switch II rigidification in GDP and GMPPCP states. Strongly supports nucleotide-state-independent conformational trapping.

claims_validated.claim: Drug-bound conformation blocks RAF RBD effector interaction
claims_validated.paper_evidence: Biochemical assay — IC50 of 9 nM for blocking KRAS-RAF interaction
claims_validated.conclusion: PRODIGY shows MRTX1133 weakens KRAS-RAF interaction by >6000-fold. 60% of interface contacts lost.

claims_validated.claim: MRTX1133 inhibits KRAS-dependent signaling (pERK, pS6, p4EBP1) and causes tumor regression
claims_validated.conclusion: Cellular signaling and in vivo efficacy cannot be modeled by structural/docking methods. However, the validated upstream mechanism provides strong structural rationale.

aggregate_results.validation_summary: 7 of 8 paper claims computationally supported (5 fully, 2 partially). Zero contradicted.

final_verdict.summary: Multi-model computational validation strongly supports the paper's claims. All 9 Tamarind Bio models converge: MRTX1133 binds the KRAS G12D Switch II pocket with low-nanomolar affinity, traps Switch I/II in an effector-incompatible conformation regardless of nucleotide state, and disrupts RAF binding by >6000-fold. Predicted binding poses match the crystal structure within 1.5-2.0 A. No computational evidence contradicts any paper claim.
confidence_factors: 4 independent docking/structure methods agree on binding site and pose
3 independent affinity estimates converge within 2x of paper value
MD simulations directly demonstrate the proposed mechanism
Cross-method structural consistency (AlphaFold vs ESMFold: 1.1 A RMSD)
WT comparison confirms structural basis for selectivity
remaining_gaps: Cellular signaling and in vivo efficacy require experimental validation
